title: pleopeltis fraseri
intro: Pleopeltis fraseri, also known as Fraser's polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Polypodiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pleopeltis fraseri include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparation of Pleopeltis fraseri typically involves drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
